audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with Proverbs/Sinai/Zion citation texture; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for cloud of witnesses, endurance, discipline, holiness, Esau, Sinai/Zion contrast, unshakable reign.

verses.restored: Therefore, since we also have so great cloud of witnesses surrounding us, laying aside every weight and the easily entangling sin/misalignment, let us run through endurance the contest set before us,
looking away to Jesus, pioneer/founder and completer of trust, who for joy set before him endured cross, despising shame, and has sat down at right hand of throne of the Divine.
For consider the one enduring such opposition by sinners/misaligned ones against himself, so that you may not grow weary, fainting in your souls/lives.
You have not yet resisted to blood, struggling against sin/misalignment,
and you have forgotten the encouragement that speaks to you as children: My child, do not regard lightly discipline of Lord, nor faint when corrected by him.
For whom Lord loves he disciplines, and he trains every child whom he receives.
Endure into discipline; the Divine deals with you as children. For what child is there whom a parent does not discipline?
But if you are without discipline, of which all have become partners, then you are illegitimate and not children.
Furthermore we had fathers of our flesh as discipliners and we respected them; shall we not much more be arranged under Father of spirits and live?
For they indeed for a few days disciplined according to what seemed good to them, but he for benefit, into sharing his holiness.
Now all discipline for the present does not seem joy but grief, yet afterward it yields peaceful fruit of righteousness/justice to those trained by it.
Therefore straighten the drooping hands and weakened knees,
and make straight tracks for your feet, so that what is lame may not be dislocated but rather healed.
Pursue peace with all and holiness, without which no one will see the Lord,
watching lest anyone fall short from grace of the Divine; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you and through it many be defiled;
lest there be any sexually immoral/misusing or profane one like Esau, who for one meal sold his birthright.
For you know that afterward, wishing to inherit blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for change of mind, though seeking it with tears.
For you have not come to what is touched and burning fire, and darkness and gloom and storm,
and trumpet sound and voice of words, which those hearing begged that no further word be added to them.
For they could not bear what was commanded: Even if a beast touches the mountain, it shall be stoned.
And so fearful was the appearance that Moses said: I am terrified and trembling.
But you have come to Mount Zion and city of living Divine, heavenly Jerusalem, and to myriads of messengers in festival gathering,
and to assembly of firstborn ones enrolled in heavens, and to Divine judge of all, and to spirits of righteous/just ones completed,
and to Jesus mediator of new covenant, and to sprinkled blood/life speaking better than Abel.
Watch that you do not refuse the One speaking. For if they did not escape refusing the one warning upon earth, much more we who turn away from the One from heavens,
whose voice shook the earth then; but now he has promised, saying: Yet once more I shake not only earth but also heaven.
And this, Yet once more, makes clear the removal of things being shaken, as things made, so that the things not shaken may remain.
Therefore, receiving an unshakable reign, let us have grace, through which we may serve the Divine acceptably with reverence and awe,
for our Divine is consuming fire.

translation_notes: Discipline language must be handled carefully; it is training toward healing and justice-fruit, not sanctification of abuse.
Sinai/Zion contrast should not be anti-Jewish; both are Israel's sacred imagery, with Hebrews emphasizing access and unshakable reign.
Healing of the lame and pursuit of peace are central pastoral aims.
safety_note: Do not use Hebrews 12 discipline language to justify abusive parenting, spiritual coercion, or suffering as inherently good. The text points toward healing, peaceful fruit of justice, grace, and unshakable life.
